primary_reason: The study analytically and numerically compares deterministic and stochastic epidemic models with identical R0 values to show how mixing structure changes final size and outbreak probabilities.
The study combines behavioural, transit, surveillance, and contact data with an SEIR simulation to quantify how behaviour changes altered influenza and COVID-19 transmission.
The study develops and evaluates a matched-cohort logistic early-warning model for healthcare-associated infection after paediatric cardiac surgery.
The article explains common interpretive errors in R0 and Rt and provides guidance on their population meaning and timing limitations.
The national surveillance analysis estimates regional R0 and Rt to characterize early COVID-19 transmission and the effects of combined control measures.
The paper develops an optical detector for cyanobacterial blooms in aquaponic water rather than an infectious-disease surveillance application with a concrete transfer route.
The study applies and contrasts exponential-growth, maximum-likelihood, and Bayesian approaches for estimating COVID-19 R0 and generation-time sensitivity.
The analysis uses time-varying reproduction-number estimation to compare heterogeneous COVID-19 control trajectories across Chinese cities.
The study estimates pathogen-specific HFMD reproduction numbers from laboratory-confirmed surveillance outbreaks and adjusts them for seroprevalence.
The paper compares renewal-equation and SEIR approaches and alternative interval assumptions to assess robustness of time-varying reproduction-number estimates.
The study builds a meteorology-informed machine-learning approach to derive influenza outbreak early-warning thresholds.
The paper concerns wind-dispersed fungal crop diseases and does not provide a concrete human or animal infectious-disease transfer route.
The retrospective study evaluates MuLBSTA as an early-warning score for severe COVID-19 illness.
The investigation combines whole-genome sequencing, phylogenetics, and reproduction-number estimation to reconstruct community COVID-19 transmission.
The paper describes deployment and alert performance of syndromic and event-based early-warning surveillance during the Hajj.
The age-structured transmission model estimates and projects the effects of tiered restrictions and alternative lockdown strategies on COVID-19 burden.
The temporal wastewater study evaluates SARS-CoV-2 RNA detection as community surveillance that preceded reported clinical cases.
The study develops and compares boosted-regression-tree forecasts for bacillary dysentery incidence and outbreak alerts across climatic regions.
The paper develops a framework showing why forward-looking serial intervals, rather than backward or intrinsic intervals, correctly link growth rates to reproduction numbers.
The hospital study combines genomic surveillance and EHR machine learning to detect a previously unrecognized Pseudomonas outbreak and its transmission route.
The paper derives reproduction-number properties for epidemic processes on semi-Markov regime-switching networks.
The data-driven simulations compare market and farm sentinel configurations for timely detection of livestock outbreaks on cattle-transport networks.
The semimechanistic Bayesian model estimates the effects of US social-distancing interventions on SARS-CoV-2 transmission and deaths.
This proof-of-concept assesses modified NEWS for rapid pre-evaluation of COVID-19 patients at risk of clinical deterioration.
The outbreak report estimates serial intervals and reproduction numbers for COVID-19 transmission in a penitentiary complex.
This delay differential-equation model examines epidemic waves under alternative temporary-immunity mechanisms.
The study evaluates and prospectively applies a whole-genome SNP framework for Salmonella Enteritidis outbreak detection and source tracing.
This multicenter study externally evaluates, augments, and calibrates a COVID-19 early-warning score.
Transmission models test whether altered reproduction numbers or cross-immunity explain anomalous influenza A and B seasonality.
The study combines contact surveys and an SEIR model to quantify how Greek distancing measures reduced SARS-CoV-2 transmission.
The compartmental model provides real-time state-level Rt estimates and adaptive forecasts to assess German COVID-19 interventions.
The simulation framework quantifies confidence that an outbreak has ended and evaluates alternative Ebola end-of-outbreak declaration rules.
The paper proposes a state-space Hawkes-process method for estimating time-varying COVID-19 reproduction numbers from daily cases.
The report maps COVID-19 transmission chains and superspreading under early testing, tracing, quarantine, and distancing measures.
This agent-based transmission model estimates vaccination effects on infections, hospitalizations, and deaths.
The provincial surveillance study estimates COVID-19 reproduction numbers under alternative generation-time assumptions.
The retracted study concerns wearable-device prediction for stroke nursing needs rather than an infectious-disease outcome.
The study uses spatial clustering and hotspot mapping of meningitis surveillance data to identify potential outbreak locations.
This concept article proposes uncertainty-aware heuristic early-warning guidance integrating infection surveillance and sepsis management.
The review identifies shedding models, back-calculation, and sampling design as key methodological bottlenecks for wastewater COVID-19 early warning.
The review compares EWARN system structures and performance indicators across emergency settings to identify implementation lessons.
The paper introduces a Monte Carlo estimator for temporal reproduction numbers when symptom-onset information is incomplete.
The review calls generally for improved infectious-disease monitoring and emergency response systems without providing substantive evaluative or prescriptive method content.
The cohort identifies and evaluates admission biomarkers and clinical predictors for early warning of COVID-19 mortality.
The SEIR model estimates when post-lockdown reproduction-number estimates stabilize using hospital, intensive-care, and death data.
The study monitors reported contacts and transmission to assess how distancing measures controlled the Brussels COVID-19 wave.
The study demonstrates bias in COVID-19 R0 estimates based on incomplete public data and re-estimates R0 using symptom-onset and tracing data.
The individual-based simulations estimate the effect of reopening Norwegian schools on SARS-CoV-2 reproduction numbers.
The study uses LASSO negative-binomial modelling of mortality and socioeconomic data to flag US counties with underdetected COVID-19 infections.
The full-scale MERS scenario exercise functionally evaluates Vietnam's outbreak early-warning and response capabilities.
Statistical and dynamic transmission models estimate the increased transmissibility and projected impact of the B.1.1.7 SARS-CoV-2 lineage.
The paper develops and evaluates a probabilistic dengue superensemble forecasting system across lead times and outbreak-detection tasks.
The retrospective evaluation measures how sequence-based typing and epidemiological linkage improve hepatitis A outbreak detection.
The analysis evaluates multiple digital traces as near-real-time early indicators of state-level COVID-19 cases and deaths.
The mixed-method assessment identifies complementary animal-health data sources and integration needs for Tanzanian early-warning surveillance.
The paper evaluates optimization and heuristic strategies for selecting outbreak-monitoring nodes in temporal contact networks using SIR simulations.
The analysis characterizes cross-border COVID-19 contact-tracing workflows, exposure contexts, and public-health workload.
The prospective school surveillance study estimates secondary attack rates and effective reproduction numbers under strict prevention measures.
The systematic review synthesizes evidence on pre-hospital early-warning scores for identifying sepsis and predicting patient outcomes.
The cross-country model estimates delayed associations between introducing or lifting non-pharmaceutical interventions and SARS-CoV-2 Rt.
The study estimates Western European COVID-19 R0 from early exponential growth and reconstructed generation intervals.
The article discusses the prospective value of artificial intelligence and online data for epidemic warning without presenting a developed or evaluated method.
The review describes wastewater SARS-CoV-2 surveillance, sampling and detection-method requirements, and implementation challenges for early warning.
The study links mobility and enterprise data to Rt to estimate mobility-reduction targets during Japan's first COVID-19 wave.
This age- and region-structured transmission model projects vaccination and intervention-relaxation scenarios.
The paper proposes and evaluates a sliding-window adaptive SIR method for continuous infection-rate and Rt estimation.
The study applies change-point detection to excess-death time series to define COVID-19 epidemic transition periods.
The modelling study fits logistic differential equations to HFMD surveillance data to derive region-specific early-warning weeks.
The machine-learning models forecast cyanobacterial blooms in a reservoir rather than an infectious-disease outcome with a concrete transfer route.
The international change-point analysis relates mobility trajectories to COVID-19 infections, mortality, and lockdown timing.
This study applies standardized early-warning indicators to national surveillance of HIV drug resistance.
The paper develops and compares genetic-algorithm-optimized neural-network models for HFMD prediction and threshold-based early warning.
The study evaluates three change-point methods through simulation and vaccine-safety data for early detection of adverse-event signals.
The retrospective study compares several early-warning scores for 48-hour mortality prediction in suspected COVID-19 patients.
The study develops, calibrates, and compares machine-learning models that augment NEWS for emergency-department septic-shock prediction.
The paper extends reproduction-number estimation to account for superspreading and derives its effect on credible-interval width.
The simulation and empirical analysis shows how changing testing and reporting practices bias COVID-19 R0 and Rt estimates.
The paper develops a mechanistic framework for dynamic social heterogeneity and distinguishes transient collective immunity from durable herd immunity.
The paper designs and implements a blockchain-based collaborative infectious-disease early-warning framework with federated and social surveillance modes.
The national CPE surveillance analysis uses temporal regression and WHONET/SaTScan cluster detection to describe emerging resistance patterns.
The comparative validation evaluates how antihypertensive use modifies qSOFA and NEWS performance for identifying sepsis and adverse outcomes.
The study estimates SARS-CoV-2 serial intervals from transmission pairs and examines variation by intervention period and incidence.
This simulation study identifies regression-to-mean bias and optimizes corrective drug-efficacy survey designs.
The prospective cohort validates an obstetric early-warning system and physiological triggers, including for puerperal infection detection.
The study combines spatial, reproduction-number, projection, and genomic analyses to characterize SARS-CoV-2 transmission in Bangladesh.
The retrospective cohort compares seven paediatric early-warning scores for critical-care admission and sepsis-related mortality among febrile children.
The paper develops a kernel-modulated spatial SIR model to reproduce multifractal COVID-19 spread from county to continental scales.
The paper introduces a nonparametric compartmental modelling class for reconstructing evolving lockdown effects and SARS-CoV-2 reproduction numbers.
The prospective study develops and evaluates a composite heart-rate-variability and biomarker model for early prediction of infection after stem-cell transplantation.
The multi-centre study compares NEWS2 and COVID-GRAM discrimination for predicting critical COVID-19 illness.
The geographical panel analysis evaluates whether Kenya's frontline epidemiology training improved measles-surveillance reporting completeness and timeliness.
The temporal wastewater study evaluates weekly SARS-CoV-2 monitoring as a cost-effective early warning for Hungary's second wave.
The paper develops and evaluates a mixed short- and long-read SNP-typing pipeline for rapid clonal outbreak detection.
The paper descriptively summarizes reported West Nile virus cases and seasonal patterns without evaluating a transferable surveillance or modelling design.
The mechanistic analysis links mask efficacy, viral abundance, infection probability, and reproduction number for SARS-CoV-2 transmission.
The mathematical model links US social-distancing policies to changing reproduction numbers and secondary-wave scenarios.
The cohort evaluates the poor sepsis-detection performance and neurological-worsening triggers of MEWS-based scores in neurocritical care.
The Bayesian change-point SEIR analysis quantifies effects of a mass gathering and movement-control order on Malaysian COVID-19 transmission.
The study assesses how surveillance-source biases and subpopulation representation alter COVID-19 Rt estimates from cases, admissions, and deaths.
The paper develops a social-contact-based approach for mapping combinations of distancing measures to COVID-19 reproduction numbers.
The retrospective imaging study identifies quantitative lung-segment CT trajectories as early warnings of severe or critical COVID-19.
The campus study evaluates targeted SARS-CoV-2 wastewater monitoring alongside testing and tracing for outbreak detection and mitigation.
The review describes wastewater and biosensor approaches for SARS-CoV-2 detection and an early-warning monitoring-system architecture.
The cluster-randomized vaccination follow-up compares gender-neutral and girls-only programmes for population-level HPV herd effects.
The cross-country analysis estimates initial COVID-19 R0 and models its associations with demographic, social, and environmental factors.
The scoping review synthesizes artificial-intelligence use cases, data sources, and operational gaps for pandemic forecasting, surveillance, diagnosis, and prognosis.
The retrospective cohort evaluates NEWS thresholds for predicting intensive-care admission among older emergency-department influenza patients.
The paper predicts online public-opinion cascade virality rather than an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer application.
The study uses a hepatitis-and-fever outbreak simulation exercise to assess peripheral health-worker preparedness for detection and response.
This prospective analysis compares early-warning scores for predicting mortality among critically ill COVID-19 patients.
The paper defines and studies reproduction numbers and stability conditions for stochastic SIR, SIS, and SEIR models with random perturbations.
The analysis combines corrected meta-analysis, contact tracing, and population testing to estimate SARS-CoV-2 household secondary attack and reproduction numbers.
The paper develops a clinic-diagnosis-based regional index and ensemble model for local influenza outbreak detection and prediction.
The paper fits structured COVID-19 epidemic models and estimates control reproduction numbers with sensitivity analysis for Shaanxi Province.
The retrospective study evaluates modified NEWS thresholds and outcome prediction for COVID-19 triage and inpatient deterioration monitoring.
The paper reviews, compares, and simulates four model-agnostic approaches for estimating disease basic reproduction numbers.
The paper derives analytical optimal-allocation conditions for limited vaccines to minimize the effective reproduction number in interacting populations.
The modified SEIR model compares cost-effective testing and distancing strategies targeted at reducing COVID-19 reproduction numbers and deaths.
The HOPE transmission model estimates US HIV effective reproduction numbers and evaluates continuum-of-care interventions for elimination.
The paper compares heterogeneous age- and region-structured epidemic models for robust estimation of influenza reproduction numbers.
The article advocates standardized genomic metadata for foodborne outbreak response without developing or evaluating a method or guidance procedure in sufficient detail.
The locally fitted SEIR-D model forecasts COVID-19 hospital demand, bed occupancy, discharges, and deaths for operational planning.
The study estimates weekly COVID-19 reproduction numbers from Saudi Arabian incidence data to monitor epidemic control.
The study develops and evaluates a circulating-microparticle and monocyte early-warning model for bloodstream infection in older patients.
The repeated contingent-valuation study evaluates how COVID-19 changed public willingness to fund an international infectious-disease early-warning system.
The paper models COVID-19-related internet public opinion rather than infection transmission, surveillance, or an infectious-disease outcome.
The Bayesian analysis corrects COVID-19 surveillance curves for case misclassification and infection-date uncertainty before estimating Rt.
The paper develops a stochastic mechanistic and data-driven Bayesian framework for reconstructing time-varying COVID-19 reproduction numbers.
This registry cohort estimates direct and indirect vaccine effectiveness using within-person pre-campaign comparisons and time-varying epidemic adjustment.
The paper estimates Italy's second-wave COVID-19 reproduction number and projects short-term cumulative and daily incidence.
The paper introduces and conceptually compares the Estimated Dissemination Ratio as a simple real-time epidemic trajectory and early-warning metric.
This meta-analysis benchmarks the prognostic accuracy, thresholds, and subgroup limitations of infection early-warning scores.
The Madrid implementation evaluates high-coverage wastewater SARS-CoV-2 surveillance against incidence and hospitalization as an early-warning tool.
The paper develops spatial stochastic and scaling models for leptospirosis vulnerability mapping, early warning, and near-real-time outbreak forecasting.
This review explains wastewater surveillance as a transferable early-outbreak detection and population-monitoring strategy for pathogenic viruses.
The contact-tracing analysis estimates serial intervals and county-level effective reproduction-number trajectories across demographic groups and reopening periods.
The review summarizes infectious-disease early-warning principles, models, systems, and multi-source big-data applications.
The systematic review compares qSOFA, NEWS, and MEWS sensitivity, specificity, and discrimination for emergency-department sepsis prognosis.
This is a prospective study protocol and reports no evaluated early-warning or surveillance findings.
The article proposes a 3Cs-based indicator framework for monitoring and early warning of vaccine hesitancy.
The review appraises wastewater-based epidemiology for COVID-19 timing, routine surveillance, and transmission-model parameterization.
The consensus statement describes Sentinel's detect-connect-empower framework for preemptive pathogen detection and pandemic response.

The study uses temporal wastewater SARS-CoV-2 RNA measurements to zone Ahmedabad into COVID-19 preparedness hotspots ahead of case changes.

The study applies multiple change-point and trend analyses to COVID-19 case and death trajectories in India and WHO regions.
The paper combines warning indicators with system-dynamics propagation modelling to simulate COVID-19 trajectories and intervention effects.
The study combines weekly contact surveys with child susceptibility assumptions to estimate the effect of English school reopening on SARS-CoV-2 R.
The mathematical model addresses cassava mosaic crop disease and provides no concrete transfer route to human or animal infectious-disease epidemiology.
